description: P0: Workspace-wide strict linting, formatting, and performance guardrails
steps: Convert single-crate Cargo.toml to Cargo workspace with [workspace] members = ['crates/*']
Move existing [lints.rust] and [lints.clippy] sections to [workspace.lints.rust] and [workspace.lints.clippy] in root Cargo.toml
Ensure workspace lint groups: clippy::all = deny, clippy::pedantic = warn, clippy::nursery = warn, clippy::cargo = warn, clippy::perf = deny

Ensure workspace lint pedantic allows: module_name_repetitions, must_use_candidate, missing_errors_doc, missing_panics_doc, items_after_statements, no_effect_underscore_binding, cast_precision_loss, cast_possible_truncation, cast_sign_loss, cast_lossless, default_trait_access, struct_excessive_bools, doc_markdown

Ensure workspace restriction lints (cherry-picked): unwrap_used=warn, expect_used=warn, panic=warn, dbg_macro=deny, todo=warn, print_stdout=warn, print_stderr=warn, undocumented_unsafe_blocks=deny, indexing_slicing=warn, unwrap_in_result=warn, clone_on_ref_ptr=warn, create_dir=warn, exit=warn
Ensure [workspace.lints.rust] unsafe_code = deny
Add [lints] workspace = true to each member crate Cargo.toml (libaipm, aipm, aipm-pack)

Verify rustfmt.toml at workspace root applies to all crates: edition 2021, max_width 100, use_small_heuristics Max, Unix newlines, trailing commas in match blocks, separated derives, field init shorthand, try shorthand, format_code_in_doc_comments

Verify clippy.toml at workspace root applies to all crates: cognitive-complexity-threshold 15, type-complexity-threshold 200, too-many-lines-threshold 100, too-large-for-stack 200, trivial-copy-size-limit 8
Verify clippy.toml test exemptions: allow-unwrap-in-tests, allow-expect-in-tests, allow-dbg-in-tests, allow-print-in-tests
Run 'cargo fmt --check' across entire workspace — zero violations
Run 'cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings' across entire workspace — zero violations
Add CI step: cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings (gate on both)

description: P0: Project scaffolding and Cargo workspace setup for two-binary architecture
steps: Create Cargo workspace root with [workspace] members = ['crates/*']
Create crates/libaipm/ — shared library crate with manifest parser, resolver, store, lockfile logic
Create crates/aipm/ — consumer binary crate (install, validate, doctor, link)
Create crates/aipm-pack/ — author binary crate (init, pack, publish, yank, login)
Configure each member Cargo.toml with [lints] workspace = true to inherit workspace linting
Add core shared dependencies in [workspace.dependencies]: clap, toml, serde, serde_json, reqwest, sha2, semver, thiserror, anyhow
Add platform-specific dependency: junction crate (Windows only) via [target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies]
Set up cucumber-rs test harness (cucumber 0.22, futures 0.3) with [[test]] name='bdd' harness=false in libaipm
Create tests/bdd.rs entry point that discovers tests/features/**/*.feature files
Verify 'cargo build --workspace' compiles both binaries
Verify 'cargo test --workspace' runs the BDD harness
Verify 'cargo fmt --check' and 'cargo clippy --workspace' pass with zero violations

description: P0: TOML manifest parser and validator for aipm.toml
steps: Define Rust structs in libaipm for manifest schema: Package, Workspace, Dependencies, Environment, Components, Features, Install, Overrides
Implement deserialization from TOML using serde + toml crate
Support [package] fields: name, version, description, type (skill|agent|mcp|hook|composite)
Support [workspace] fields: members glob patterns, plugins_dir (default 'claude-plugins')
Support [dependencies] with version strings ('^1.0'), workspace refs ({workspace = '^'}), optional deps ({version, optional = true})
Support [workspace.dependencies] catalog section and [catalog]/[catalogs.*] named catalogs
Support [components] section: skills, agents, hooks, mcp_servers arrays
Support [environment] section: requires, aipm version, platforms, variables, runtime
Support [overrides] section with path-scoped overrides
Support [install] section: allowed_build_scripts allowlist
Support [package].files allowlist for transfer format
Implement validation: required fields present, name format valid, version is valid semver, component paths exist on disk
Return structured errors with file positions for AI-friendly diagnostics
Wire into both aipm and aipm-pack binaries via libaipm
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/manifest/validation.feature

description: P0: aipm-pack init — package initialization and scaffolding
steps: Implement 'aipm-pack init' command with clap
Support --type flag: skill, agent, mcp, hook, composite
Generate aipm.toml with [package] section populated (name from directory, version 0.1.0)
Create conventional directory layout based on type: skills/, agents/, hooks/, mcp/ directories
Generate starter component files (e.g. SKILL.md template for skill type)
Support init at repo root: generate [workspace] section with members = ['claude-plugins/*']
Support init inside plugins_dir: generate [package] section only (member manifest)
Validate naming conventions: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, scoped names (@org/name)
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/manifest/init.feature

description: P0: Semver parsing, version ranges, and pre-release handling
steps: Implement semver parser supporting major.minor.patch format
Support caret ranges (^1.0 = >=1.0.0 <2.0.0) as the default strategy
Support tilde ranges (~1.0 = >=1.0.0 <1.1.0)
Support exact pinning (=1.0.0)
Support wildcard ranges (1.x, 1.0.x)
Support pre-release versions (1.0.0-beta.1) with correct ordering
Implement version compatibility checking (does version X satisfy range Y)
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/manifest/versioning.feature

description: P0: Content-addressable global store with SHA-512 hashing
steps: Implement global store at ~/.aipm/store/ (configurable via env var or config)
Hash file contents with SHA-512, use 2-char prefix directories (e.g. ab/cd1234...)
Implement store_file(content) -> hash: write to store if not exists, return hash
Implement has_file(hash) -> bool: check if content already stored
Implement get_file_path(hash) -> Path: resolve hash to filesystem path
Handle concurrent access safely (atomic writes via temp file + rename)
Implement store statistics: total files, total size, deduplication ratio
Test deduplication: storing same content twice produces one file

description: P0: Link manager — symlinks (macOS/Linux) and directory junctions (Windows)
steps: Implement link_plugin_dir(source, target) with platform-specific behavior
On macOS/Linux: use std::os::unix::fs::symlink for directory links
On Windows: use junction::create for directory junctions (no elevation needed)
Implement hard-link creation for individual files (.aipm/links/ -> global store)
Implement gitignore management: add/remove entries from claude-plugins/.gitignore
Preserve manual entries in .gitignore (only manage aipm-added entries)
Implement link removal (unlink symlink/junction and remove gitignore entry)
Test on Windows: verify junction works without Developer Mode or elevation
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/install.feature (linking scenarios)

description: P0: Dependency resolution — backtracking constraint solver
steps: Build dependency graph from root manifest + all workspace members
Implement highest-version-first candidate selection
Implement version unification within same semver-major (one version per major)
Implement cross-major coexistence: ^1.0 and ^2.0 of same pkg both resolve
Implement backtracking on conflict within same major range
Apply [overrides] before resolution (forced versions bypass normal solving)
Exclude yanked versions unless pinned in existing lockfile
Handle cross-type dependencies: skill depending on mcp server, etc.
Generate resolved dependency tree with exact versions and integrity hashes
Implement minimal reconciliation: when manifest changes, only resolve new/changed deps, keep existing pins
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/dependencies/resolution.feature

description: P0: Lockfile creation and management (aipm.lock)
steps: Define lockfile TOML schema: [metadata] section with lockfile_version, generated_by
Implement [[package]] entries: name, version, source, checksum (SHA-512), dependencies
Write lockfile deterministically: sorted packages, consistent formatting
Read existing lockfile and use locked versions when available
Implement minimal reconciliation: on manifest change, resolve only new/changed entries, preserve existing pins
Implement --locked mode: fail immediately if aipm.lock doesn't match aipm.toml (zero drift tolerance)
Implement update command: aipm update [pkg] re-resolves to latest compatible versions
Ensure lockfile is human-readable for meaningful git diffs
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/dependencies/lockfile.feature

description: P0: aipm install — full install flow
steps: Implement 'aipm install [pkg[@version]]' command with clap
Step 1: Resolve dependencies using backtracking solver (or lockfile if present)
Step 2: Fetch .aipm archives not in global store from registry
Step 3: Verify SHA-512 integrity against registry index before extracting
Step 4: Extract files into global store by content hash (skip existing = dedup)
Step 5: Assemble .aipm/links/<pkg>/ directories with hard links from global store
Step 6: Create directory links in claude-plugins/ -> .aipm/links/ (symlink or junction)
Step 7: Add installed packages to claude-plugins/.gitignore
Step 8: Update aipm.toml [dependencies] if installing a new package
Step 9: Update aipm.lock with exact versions + integrity hashes
Implement --locked flag for CI mode (fail on drift)
Implement --offline flag (install from cache only, no network)
Implement strict isolation: only declared deps accessible, transitive deps hidden
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/install.feature

description: P0: aipm update — explicit version upgrade
steps: Implement 'aipm update [pkg]' command
With package name: re-resolve only that package to latest compatible version
Without package name: re-resolve all dependencies to latest compatible versions
Update aipm.lock with new versions
Re-run install flow for changed packages (fetch, store, link)
This is the ONLY command that upgrades locked versions

description: P0: aipm uninstall — remove a dependency
steps: Implement 'aipm uninstall <pkg>' command
Remove package from [dependencies] in aipm.toml
Remove directory link from claude-plugins/
Remove entry from claude-plugins/.gitignore
Remove package from aipm.lock
Clean up .aipm/links/ (remove hard-linked directory)
Do NOT remove from global store (may be used by other projects)

description: P0: aipm validate — manifest and component validation
steps: Implement 'aipm validate' command
Parse and validate aipm.toml against schema
Verify all component paths in [components] exist on disk
Check skill files have valid SKILL.md format
Check hook files are valid JSON
Check MCP server configs are valid
Check environment dependencies (tools on PATH, env vars set)
Return structured error output with file positions
Support --json flag for machine-readable output
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/manifest/validation.feature

description: P0: aipm-pack pack — create .aipm archive (transfer format)
steps: Implement 'aipm-pack pack [--dry-run]' command
Step 1: Validate aipm.toml is valid and required components exist
Step 2: Normalize manifest: resolve workspace:^ refs to concrete versions, expand catalog: refs
Step 3: Apply [package].files allowlist, exclude secrets/.git/build artifacts by default
Step 4: Create gzip-compressed tar with deterministic ordering (sorted files, zeroed timestamps)
Step 5: Compute SHA-512 of archive, embed in checksum.sha512
Step 6: Output package-name-version.aipm file
Implement --dry-run: report files that would be included and archive size without creating
Enforce max archive size (default 10MB, configurable)
Implement secrets detection in pre-pack validation
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/publish.feature (pack-related scenarios)

description: P0: aipm-pack publish — publish to registry
steps: Implement 'aipm-pack publish [--dry-run]' command
Run pack flow first to create .aipm archive
Authenticate with registry using stored bearer token
Upload .aipm archive via PUT /api/v1/packages/{name}/{version}
Handle registry responses: success, duplicate version (immutable), auth failure, size limit
Implement --dry-run: run pack + validate but don't upload
Support scoped packages (@org/name): verify org membership/permissions
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/publish.feature

description: P0: aipm-pack yank — yank published versions
steps: Implement 'aipm-pack yank <pkg@version>' command
Send yank request to registry API
Yanked versions excluded from new resolutions but still available for existing lockfiles
Support un-yank to reverse the operation
Never allow deletion — versions are permanent (Cargo model)
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/yank.feature

description: P0: Registry authentication — aipm-pack login/logout
steps: Implement 'aipm-pack login' command
Prompt for API token or accept via --token flag
Store tokens in OS credential store (not plaintext files)
Support per-registry tokens for multi-registry setups
Implement 'aipm-pack logout' command to clear stored tokens
Implement token validation on login (test against registry API)

description: P0: Local + registry plugin coexistence
steps: Support local plugins as git-tracked directories in claude-plugins/
Support registry plugins as directory links in claude-plugins/ (gitignored)
Local plugins can declare registry dependencies in their own aipm.toml
Support non-workspace single-package mode (aipm.toml without [workspace])
Manage claude-plugins/.gitignore: add symlinked installs, preserve manual entries
Support vendoring: aipm vendor <pkg> copies registry package into claude-plugins/ as git-tracked
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/local-and-registry.feature

description: P0: aipm link / unlink — local development overrides
steps: Implement 'aipm link <path>' command in consumer binary
Replace registry dependency's directory link with link to local directory
Do NOT modify lockfile or manifest (override is ephemeral)
Validate that linked directory contains valid aipm.toml
Implement 'aipm unlink <pkg>' to restore original registry symlink
Implement 'aipm list --linked' to show all active link overrides
'aipm install --locked' removes all links and restores registry versions (CI-safe)
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/link.feature

description: P0: Registry client — HTTP client for registry API
steps: Implement HTTP client using reqwest for registry communication
Support multi-registry with scoped routing (@org/* -> org registry URL)
Implement package fetch: GET /api/v1/packages/{name}/{version}
Implement package index query: GET /api/v1/packages/{name}/versions
Implement package upload: PUT /api/v1/packages/{name}/{version}
Implement yank/un-yank: PATCH /api/v1/packages/{name}/{version}
Handle authentication: bearer token per registry
Handle errors: network failures, auth failures, not found, rate limiting
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/security.feature (auth scenarios)

description: P1: Workspace protocol — workspace:^ references
steps: Support { workspace = '^' } dependency syntax in member manifests
Resolve workspace refs to actual sibling package versions during install
On aipm-pack publish: replace workspace:^ with concrete version (e.g. ^2.3.0)
On aipm-pack publish: replace workspace:= with exact version (e.g. =2.3.0)
Validate that workspace ref targets exist as workspace members
Pass relevant cucumber scenarios in tests/features/monorepo/orchestration.feature

description: P1: Dependency catalogs — shared version ranges across workspace
steps: Support [catalog] section in root aipm.toml for default catalog
Support [catalogs.<name>] sections for named catalogs (stable, next, etc.)
Support 'catalog:' dependency syntax in member manifests to reference catalog versions
Support 'catalog:<name>' for named catalog references
On aipm-pack publish: expand catalog: refs to concrete version ranges
Validate catalog references exist in root manifest
Pass relevant cucumber scenarios in tests/features/monorepo/orchestration.feature

description: P1: Workspace filtering — --filter flag for scoped commands
steps: Implement --filter flag for workspace commands
Support name globs: --filter 'skill-*'
Support path patterns: --filter './claude-plugins/my-*'
Support git-diff selectors: --filter '[origin/main]' (changed packages only)
Support dependency graph traversal: --filter '...skill-a' (skill-a and its dependents)
Implement 'aipm build --filter <pattern>' command
Pass relevant cucumber scenarios in tests/features/monorepo/orchestration.feature

description: P1: Optional features system — conditional dependencies
steps: Support [features] section in manifest with named feature groups
Support 'default' feature list
Support 'dep:' syntax for optional dependency activation
Implement additive feature unification across dependency graph (Cargo model)
Resolve optional dependencies only when their feature is activated
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/dependencies/features.feature

description: P1: Built-in dependency patching
steps: Implement 'aipm patch <pkg@version>' command: extract package to temp dir for editing
Implement 'aipm patch-commit <dir>' command: generate .patch file from modifications
Store patches in patches/ directory (git tracked)
Apply patches during install after extracting from store
Validate patches still apply cleanly on version updates (warn on conflicts)
Support removing patches
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/dependencies/patching.feature

description: P1: Dependency overrides — path-scoped version forcing
steps: Support [overrides] section in root manifest
Support simple overrides: 'vulnerable-lib' = '^2.0.0'
Support path-scoped overrides: 'skill-a>common-util' = '=2.1.0'
Apply overrides before dependency resolution
Warn when overrides are active (they bypass normal resolution safety)
Pass relevant cucumber scenarios in tests/features/dependencies/resolution.feature

description: P1: Compositional reuse — composite packages with cross-type dependencies
steps: Support type = 'composite' packages that contain multiple component types
Support cross-type dependencies: skill depending on mcp server, hook depending on skill
Validate component type consistency (hook package must contain valid hook JSON, etc.)
Support [components] section listing skills, agents, hooks, mcp_servers
Ensure Claude Code discovers all components via directory scanning
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/reuse/compositional-reuse.feature

description: P1: Environment dependency declarations and aipm doctor
steps: Support [environment].requires for system tool requirements (git, docker, etc.)
Support [environment].aipm for minimum aipm version requirement
Support [environment].platforms for supported platform constraints
Support [environment.variables] for required/optional env var declarations
Support [environment.runtime] for runtime version requirements (node >= 18)
Implement 'aipm doctor' command: check all environment requirements across all installed packages
Generate summary report showing met/missing requirements with suggested fixes
Warn on install when environment requirements are missing (fail in strict mode)
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/environment/dependencies.feature

description: P1: aipm search — registry package discovery
steps: Implement 'aipm search <query>' command
Search by name, description, and tags
Support filtering by component type (--type skill|agent|mcp|hook|composite)
Display results with name, version, description, download count
Support --json flag for machine-readable output
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/search.feature

description: P1: aipm info and aipm list — package inspection
steps: Implement 'aipm info <pkg>' command: display package details from registry
Show name, version, description, dependencies, components, environment requirements
Implement 'aipm list' command: show all installed packages
Implement 'aipm list --outdated': show packages with newer versions available
Implement 'aipm list --linked': show active link overrides
Support --json flag for machine-readable output

description: P1: aipm audit — security vulnerability checking
steps: Implement 'aipm audit' command
Check installed packages against advisory database
Report known vulnerabilities with severity and remediation advice
Support --json flag for CI integration
Warn on install when installing packages with known vulnerabilities
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/registry/security.feature

description: P1: aipm vendor — copy registry package to local
steps: Implement 'aipm vendor <pkg>' command
Copy registry package into claude-plugins/ as a git-tracked directory
Convert from installed (symlink) to vendored (real directory)
Vendored package becomes workspace member with its own aipm.toml
Remove symlink and gitignore entry for the vendored package

description: P1: Monorepo orchestration integration
steps: Support [workspace] members with glob patterns
Support virtual workspace (root has [workspace] but no [package])
Single lockfile at workspace root for all members
Support dependency inheritance from [workspace.dependencies]
Detect and handle circular workspace dependencies
Support lifecycle hooks for orchestrator integration (Rush, Turborepo, etc.)
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/monorepo/orchestration.feature

description: P1: Cross-platform self-contained binary distribution
steps: Set up CI/CD for building release binaries: linux-x64, linux-arm64, macos-x64, macos-arm64, windows-x64
Configure cross-compilation or per-platform build agents
Create GitHub release artifacts for each platform
Implement version self-check: aipm --version
Test binary works without any runtime dependencies on each platform
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/portability/cross-stack.feature

description: P1: Offline mode — install from cache only
steps: Implement 'aipm install --offline' flag
Resolve and install using only packages available in global store
Fail with clear error if required package not in cache
Useful for air-gapped CI environments

description: P1: Lifecycle script blocking and side-effects cache
steps: Block dependency lifecycle scripts by default (pnpm v10 model)
Support [install].allowed_build_scripts allowlist in root manifest
Cache lifecycle script outputs in global store (~/.aipm/store/)
Reuse cached side-effects on subsequent installs (skip recompilation)
Warn when a dependency declares lifecycle scripts that are blocked

description: P1: Parallel operations — concurrent resolve, fetch, and link
steps: Implement concurrent package fetching (download multiple archives in parallel)
Implement concurrent file hashing and store operations
Implement concurrent hard-link creation for .aipm/links/
Use pnpm three-stage pipeline model: resolve -> fetch -> link (overlapping stages)
Add progress indicators for long-running operations

description: P1: Structured output and verbose mode
steps: Implement --json flag on all commands for machine-readable CI/CD output
Implement --verbose flag showing resolution decisions, fetch progress, link operations
Ensure error output includes structured diagnostics for AI agent consumption
Human-readable lockfile TOML enables meaningful git diffs

description: P1: JSON Schema publication for IDE support
steps: Create JSON Schema for aipm.toml manifest format
Submit schema to SchemaStore.org
Verify Taplo LSP picks up the schema for VS Code autocomplete
Verify schema validates all manifest fields including nested sections

description: P2: aipm-pack lint — quality checks for AI components
steps: Implement 'aipm-pack lint [--fix]' command
Validate skill files against best practice standards
Validate agent definitions for completeness
Validate hook configurations for correctness
Validate MCP server definitions
Generate quality scores for packages
Support --fix flag for auto-fixable issues
Return machine-readable errors for AI agent consumption
Pass cucumber scenarios in tests/features/guardrails/quality.feature
